title: CIL Geology — Advanced Mock 15 (Conceptual)
pattern: CIL MT — 200 MCQ · 2 papers · 1 mark each · no negative marking

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which Indian state shares borders with the maximum number of states?
questions.solution: Uttar Pradesh (9)

questions.solution: H₂SO₄

questions.stem: A box has 12 pens: 4 defective. 3 drawn at random. Probability at least one defective:

questions.solution: 10 kg

questions.solution: 5 days

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Pipes A and B fill in 18 and 24 min. Pipe C empties at same rate as A fills. All opened, time to fill:
questions.solution: 36 min

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Find the smallest number which leaves remainder 8 when divided by 12, 18, and 24:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A man covers distance at 40 km/h. If he increases by 25%, time reduces by 30 min. Distance:
questions.solution: 100 km

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A can do a work in 20 days, B in 30 days. They work together for 5 days, then A leaves. B finishes remaining in:
questions.solution: 17.5 days

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Two articles are sold for ₹990 each. One at 10% profit, other at 10% loss. Net profit/loss:
questions.solution: 1% loss

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A man divides his property among 4 sons in ratio 1/2 : 1/3 : 1/4 : 1/5. The least share is to total:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability

questions.solution: 20 days

questions.solution: 48 km/h

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Six children A-G (D missing) sit in circle. A faces B. C is left of A. E is right of B. F is between A and E. Who is opposite F?
questions.solution: A

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Five boxes stacked. Box 1 below Box 3. Box 4 above Box 1. Box 2 below Box 5. Box 3 above Box 5. Which is bottommost?
questions.solution: Box 2

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statement: CIL's profits have declined for three consecutive quarters. Conclusion I: CIL is incurring losses. II: CIL's operational costs have increased.
questions.solution: Neither follows

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A team of 3 is to be formed from 5 engineers. Two specific engineers refuse to serve together. Number of possible teams:

questions.stem: From point P, Q is 16 m east. R is 12 m south of Q. Distance P to R:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: At what time between 8 and 9 are hands opposite?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a circle of 8, M is opposite N. O is 2nd right of M. P is adjacent to N. Q is between O and M. Who is 2nd left of P?
questions.solution: O

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Six cars in a row: Honda left of Toyota. Ford right of BMW. Audi left of Ford. Tesla right of Toyota. Which is leftmost?
questions.solution: Honda

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statement: The passing percentage in the exam was 85%, the highest in a decade. Conclusion I: The exam was easier this year. II: Students worked harder this year.
questions.solution: Neither follows

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a factory, 12 workers produce 60 units/day. Each worker produces equally. How many workers needed for 100 units/day?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A walks 30 m north, turns right 40 m, right 50 m, right 40 m. Distance from start:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: How many degrees does hour hand move in 25 min?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: 7 chairs in a row. P sits 3rd from left. Q sits 2nd right of P. R sits left of Q. S sits between P and R. Who sits 2nd from right?
questions.solution: Q

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a line of 25, Anand is ahead of Bimal. Chand is behind Bimal but ahead of Dinesh. Eeshwar is behind Dinesh. Minimum people between Anand and Dinesh:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statement: Countries investing in renewable energy have reduced their carbon emissions. Conclusion I: All countries should invest in renewable energy. II: Renewable energy reduces carbon footprint.
questions.solution: Only II follows

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A transport company must send 8 trucks. 3 are needed for emergency. Only 5 are available. How many combinations possible?

questions.stem: Find the next: 1, 4, 27, 256, ?

questions.stem: If MINERAL = 720, COAL = 360, ROCK = ?

questions.stem: Seismology : Earthquake :: Geology : ?
questions.solution: Earth

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statements: All mangoes are fruits. No fruit is vegetable. Conclusions: I. No mango is vegetable. II. Some vegetables are mangoes.
questions.solution: Only I follows

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the wrong term: C, F, J, N, Q, U
questions.solution: N

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statements: All diamonds are minerals. Some minerals are metals. No metal is a gem. Conclusions: I. Some diamonds are gems. II. Some minerals are not gems.
questions.solution: Only II follows

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In code, 1 = January, 2 = February ... then 9-10-7-1 = ?
questions.solution: JIGA

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In code, 'equal rights' = rt eq, 'right now' = nw rt, 'now or never' = nw or nr. Code for 'equal':
questions.solution: eq

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the missing: 1, 0, 3, 2, 5, 4, ?

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: The Industrial Revolution, which began in Britain in the late 18th century, was powered largely by coal. Steam engines, fuelled by coal, drove factories, railways, and ships, transforming economies and societies. The demand for coal led to the expansion of mining operations across Europe and America. However, the social cost was high, with poor working conditions, child labour, and environmental pollution. The legacy of the Industrial Revolution continues to influence modern industrial practices and environmental policies. Today, many former mining regions are being revitalised through diversification and clean energy initiatives.

Former mining regions are being revitalised through:
questions.solution: Diversification and clean energy initiatives

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: The furniture (A) / in this room (B) / are very (C) / expensive. (D)
questions.solution: C - should be 'is very'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: India's energy demand is projected to grow at 3-4% annually over the next decade. While coal remains the dominant source, accounting for about 55% of installed capacity, renewable energy is expanding rapidly. Solar power capacity has grown from just 2.6 GW in 2014 to over 70 GW in 2024. Wind power contributes another 45 GW. The government's Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for solar manufacturing aims to reduce dependence on imports. Nuclear power, with 7.5 GW capacity, and hydropower, with 47 GW, are other important components of India's energy portfolio.

Coal accounts for about what percentage of India's installed capacity?

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: Each of (A) / the participants (B) / were given (C) / a certificate. (D)
questions.solution: C - should be 'was given'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: Coal is the most abundant fossil fuel in India and plays a crucial role in the country's energy mix. India has the fourth-largest coal reserves in the world, with an estimated 344 billion tonnes. Coal India Limited (CIL) is the largest coal-producing company globally, accounting for about 82% of India's coal output. However, concerns about environmental degradation and climate change have prompted a shift towards cleaner energy sources like solar and wind power. The government has set a target of achieving 500 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2030.

According to the passage, India has the ____ largest coal reserves in the world.
questions.solution: fourth

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: He is (A) / more smarter (B) / than his (C) / elder brother. (D)
questions.solution: B - should be 'smarter'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: Underground mining is one of the most hazardous occupations in the world. The primary risks include roof falls, gas explosions, flooding, and ventilation failures. In India, the Mines Act of 1952 and the Coal Mines Regulations of 2017 govern safety standards. Modern technology has significantly improved mine safety through real-time monitoring systems, gas detectors, and automated equipment. Despite these advances, safety remains a critical concern, and regular training programs are mandatory for all mine workers. The introduction of continuous miners and longwall mining methods has increased both productivity and safety.

The Coal Mines Regulations were enacted in:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: The team (A) / are working (B) / on the new (C) / project since May. (D)
questions.solution: B - should be 'has been working'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: The Gondwana coal fields of eastern India contain the country's best quality coal. These fields stretch across Jharkhand, West Bengal, Odisha, and Chhattisgarh. The coal found here is primarily bituminous, which has high calorific value and low ash content. In contrast, the Tertiary coal fields of northeastern India contain lignite and sub-bituminous coal. Coal grading is based on ash content, with higher grades having lower ash percentages. Washeries are used to reduce ash content and improve coal quality before it is supplied to power plants and industries.

Coal grading is primarily based on:
questions.solution: Ash content

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: Neither he (A) / nor his brother (B) / are going (C) / to the party. (D)
questions.solution: C - should be 'is going'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: The Industrial Revolution, which began in Britain in the late 18th century, was powered largely by coal. Steam engines, fuelled by coal, drove factories, railways, and ships, transforming economies and societies. The demand for coal led to the expansion of mining operations across Europe and America. However, the social cost was high, with poor working conditions, child labour, and environmental pollution. The legacy of the Industrial Revolution continues to influence modern industrial practices and environmental policies. Today, many former mining regions are being revitalised through diversification and clean energy initiatives.

The word 'legacy' in the passage most nearly means:
questions.solution: Heritage

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: I am looking (A) / forward to (B) / meet you (C) / next week. (D)
questions.solution: C - should be 'meeting'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: Coal is the most abundant fossil fuel in India and plays a crucial role in the country's energy mix. India has the fourth-largest coal reserves in the world, with an estimated 344 billion tonnes. Coal India Limited (CIL) is the largest coal-producing company globally, accounting for about 82% of India's coal output. However, concerns about environmental degradation and climate change have prompted a shift towards cleaner energy sources like solar and wind power. The government has set a target of achieving 500 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2030.

What percentage of India's coal output does CIL account for?
questions.solution: About 82%

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: She is (A) / the tallest (B) / of the two (C) / sisters. (D)
questions.solution: B - should be 'taller'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: Coal is the most abundant fossil fuel in India and plays a crucial role in the country's energy mix. India has the fourth-largest coal reserves in the world, with an estimated 344 billion tonnes. Coal India Limited (CIL) is the largest coal-producing company globally, accounting for about 82% of India's coal output. However, concerns about environmental degradation and climate change have prompted a shift towards cleaner energy sources like solar and wind power. The government has set a target of achieving 500 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2030.

The government's renewable energy target by 2030 is ____ GW.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: The scenery (A) / of the mountains (B) / are (C) / breathtaking. (D)
questions.solution: C - should be 'is'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To face the music':
questions.solution: To face consequences

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of MUNDANE:
questions.solution: Extraordinary

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: The internet has revolutionised communication. Q: People can now connect instantly across the globe. R: Social media platforms enable sharing of information. S: However, digital divide remains a concern.
questions.solution: PQRS

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The organisation ____ to improve its bottom line.
questions.solution: aims

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The furniture in this room are expensive.
options: The furniture in this room are expensive.
The furnitures in this room are expensive.
The furniture in this room is expensive.
The furniture in this room were expensive.
questions.solution: The furniture in this room is expensive.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The mine was ____ after the accident.
questions.solution: sealed

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The pair of shoes are kept in the cupboard.
options: The pair of shoes is kept in the cupboard.
A pair of shoes are kept in the cupboard.
The pair of shoes are kept in the cupboard.
The pairs of shoes is kept in the cupboard.
questions.solution: The pair of shoes is kept in the cupboard.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of EXUBERANT:
questions.solution: Lethargic

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of LETHARGIC:
questions.solution: Sluggish

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Graptolite rhabdosome: growth direction
options: From sicula (first zooid) downward. Stipe growth: scandent (graptoloid) or dendroid
questions.solution: Graptolite: sicula → stipe growth. scandent: stipes merge (Monograptus). Dendroid: branching.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Ammonite zonation is used for:
questions.solution: Ammonites: rapid evolution, wide distribution. Excellent index fossils for Jurassic-Cretaceous marine rocks.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A P-wave travels at 6 km/s and arrives 20 s after the earthquake. The (approx.) distance to the source is:
questions.solution: distance = velocity × time = 6×20 = 120 km.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Evaporite sequence (bottom to top):
questions.solution: Evaporite: decreasing solubility upward. Ca-carbonate first, then Ca-sulfate (gypsum), NaCl (halite), KCl (potash).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cross-bedding:
options: Inclined layers within horizontal strata. Wind or water current direction
questions.solution: Cross-bedding: dune migration. Paleocurrent indicator.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Von Mises stress criterion predicts yielding when:
options: Second deviatoric stress invariant reaches critical value
questions.solution: Von Mises: √(J₂) = k. J₂ = second invariant of deviatoric stress. Smooth criterion, good for ductile metals.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Dunite:
questions.solution: Dunite: light green, massive. Altered to serpentinite.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Pseudosection modeling:
options: Thermodynamic phase diagram for specific bulk composition. Perple_X, Theriak-Domino. Isopleths for P-T
questions.solution: Pseudosection: modes + compositions of phases. Contours of T/P. Compare with natural assemblage.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: NDVI uses:
questions.solution: NDVI = (NIR - Red)/(NIR + Red). High = healthy vegetation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Marine transgression vs regression:
options: Transgression: landward shoreline shift (retrogradational). Regression: basinward (progradational)
questions.solution: Transgression: sea-level rise + sediment supply < accommodation.Ravinement surface.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A mineral specimen of mass 81 g has a volume of 20 cm³. Its specific gravity is about:
questions.solution: SG = mass/volume = 81/20 = 4.05.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Slate is the low-grade metamorphic product of:
questions.solution: Fine-grained shale metamorphoses to slate, with characteristic slaty cleavage.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cleat system in coal:
options: Natural fractures perpendicular to bedding. Face cleats (primary) and butt cleats (secondary)
questions.solution: Cleats: orthogonal system. Control permeability. Spacing: mm to m. Face cleats continuous.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Plate boundary triple junctions: RRR type (ridge-ridge-ridge):
options: All three plates separating. Example: Azores, Afar
questions.solution: Triple junction: RRR stable (receding). TTT stable (subducting). RRR at Afar: Red Sea, Gulf of Aden, Rift.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Isostasy principle states:
options: Crust floats on denser mantle in gravitational equilibrium
questions.solution: Airy: mountain roots. Pratt: lateral density. Glacial rebound confirms.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Conodont elements: what animal?
questions.solution: Conodonts: primitive chordate. Elements used for biostratigraphy. CAI = thermal maturity.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Mass extinction events are identified by:
options: Sharp decrease in biodiversity in fossil record
questions.solution: Mass extinction: >75% species loss in <1 Myr. 5 major events identified. Sharp drop in diversity in stratigraphic record.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Heavy minerals concentrated by flowing water in stream beds form a:
questions.solution: Placers (e.g. of gold, cassiterite) form where moving water sorts dense, durable grains.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Samarium-147 decays to:
questions.solution: Sm-Nd dating: 147Sm → 143Nd. Half-life ~106 Ga. Good for mafic/ultramafic rocks. Less affected by metamorphism.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Isograd: line on map connecting points of same metamorphic grade. Defined by:
options: First appearance of index mineral (e.g., garnet, staurolite)
questions.solution: Isograd: reaction isograd. Garnet-in, staurolite-in, kyanite-in. Maps P-T conditions.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Exsolution lamellae in feldspar indicate:
questions.solution: Exsolution: two feldspar phases separate on cooling. Perthite: K-feldspar + plagioclase lamellae. Indicates slow cooling.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Peninsular Gneiss:
options: Banded gneissic complex in Dharwar. TTG composition. 3.4-3.0 Ga
questions.solution: Peninsular Gneiss: basement for Dharwar. Multiple deformation events. Migmatitic.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: To extract 15 m³ of coal, 45 m³ of overburden must be removed. The stripping ratio is:
questions.solution: Stripping ratio = overburden/coal = 45/15 = 3 : 1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ophiolite sequence:
options: Oceanic lithosphere obducted onto continent. Harzburgite → layered gabbro → sheeted dike → pillow lava → pelagic sediment
questions.solution: Ophiolite: Oman, Troodos (Cyprus). Moho transition zone. SSZ (supra-subduction zone) ophiolite.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Blueschist facies minerals:
questions.solution: Blueschist: HP/LT subduction. Glaucophane: diagnostic blue amphibole. Jadeite: Na-pyroxene.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Shotcrete:
options: Sprayed concrete (dry or wet mix). Steel fiber reinforced. Applied to rock surface
questions.solution: Shotcrete: immediate support. Add accelerator. Wire mesh or fiber. 50-100mm thick.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hydraulic testing: packer tests isolate:
options: Specific depth intervals in borehole for permeability measurement
questions.solution: Packer test: inflatable packers seal interval. Pump or slug test in isolated zone.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Crystal twinning: contact twin vs penetration twin:
options: Contact: shared plane (mirror). Penetration: shared point (interpenetrating)
questions.solution: Contact: staurolite, gypsum. Penetration: fluorite, pyrite, garnet twins.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A mineral has passed through 2 half-lives of an isotope whose half-life is 1.3 (in the given time units). Its age is:
questions.solution: Age = number of half-lives × half-life = 2×1.3 = 2.6.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Dolomite:
options: CaMg(CO3)2 — forms by Mg replacement of calcite
questions.solution: Dolomite: effervesces weakly in cold HCl. CaMg(CO3)2.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Mud volcano:
options: Ejection of mud and gas from depth. Overpressured sediments. Often in accretionary wedges
questions.solution: Mud volcano: methane, water, fine sediment. Onshore (Azerbaijan) and offshore. Cone-shaped.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Vegetation indices — EVI:
options: Enhanced Vegetation Index. EVI = 2.5×(NIR-R)/(NIR+6R-7.5B+1). Better for high biomass
questions.solution: EVI: reduces atmospheric and soil noise. For dense vegetation (Amazon). MODIS EVI product.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sapping (spring) erosion:
options: Groundwater outflow → headward erosion. Box canyon, amphitheater valleys. Colorado Plateau
questions.solution: Sapping: chemical weathering at spring outlets. Canyon head propagation. Piping erosion.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Isostasy:
options: Buoyant equilibrium of Earth's crust. Airy (variable root depth) vs Pratt (variable density)
questions.solution: Airy: mountains have deep roots (like iceberg). Pratt: density varies laterally. Flexural isostasy.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Thermokarst:
options: Irregular surface from thawing of ice-rich permafrost. Subsidence, ponds
questions.solution: Thermokarst: alases (depressions), baydzherakhs (soil pedestals). Climate warming.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Piping in loess:
options: Subsurface erosion (tunnel erosion) in wind-deposited silt. Vertical permeability → collapse
questions.solution: Loess: metastable structure. Piping: rapid erosion along preferential flow paths.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Coastal — barrier island:
options: Long, narrow sand island parallel to coast. Lagoon behind. Inlet cuts
questions.solution: Barrier: wave-dominated. Dune ridge, beach, washover fans. Sea-level rise → barrier transgression.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: GIS components:
options: Hardware, software, data, people, methods. Spatial data: raster (grid) and vector (points, lines, polygons)
questions.solution: GIS: capture, store, analyze, display spatial data. ArcGIS, QGIS.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Phyllite as engineering material:
options: Smooth cleavage planes → low friction. Slake-prone
questions.solution: Phyllite: foliated, anisotropic. Weak along cleavage. Weeps along foliation planes.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Isotope ratios: εNd positive = depleted mantle source. εNd negative =:
options: Enriched mantle or continental crustal source
questions.solution: εNd = ((¹⁴³Nd/¹⁴⁴Nd)_sample/(¹⁴⁴Nd/¹⁴⁴Nd)_CHUR - 1) × 10⁴. Positive: depleted. Negative: enriched/crustal.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Closepet Granite:
options: Granite batholith between E and W Dharwar Cratons. ~2.5 Ga. Late Archean
questions.solution: Closepet: N-S trending. High-K, calc-alkaline. Anatectic origin. Mantle+crustal source.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Provenance analysis — heavy mineral assemblage:
options: Ultra-stable (zircon, tourmaline, rutile) vs metastable (garnet, staurolite, kyanite)
questions.solution: ZTR index (zircon-tourmaline-rutile): high = mature, recycled sediment.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Metamorphic facies:
options: P-T conditions: Zeolite, Greenschist, Amphibolite, Granulite, Blueschist, Eclogite
questions.solution: Barrovian: greenschist→amphibolite→granulite (increasing T). Blueschist: high P/low T.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A P-wave travels at 8 km/s and arrives 20 s after the earthquake. The (approx.) distance to the source is:
questions.solution: distance = velocity × time = 8×20 = 160 km.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Stratabound vs stratiform ore deposits:
options: Stratabound: confined to specific horizon. Stratiform: bedding-parallel, syngenetic
questions.solution: Stratabound: epigenetic but stratigraphically controlled. Stratiform: syngenetic, within bedding.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sm-Nd dating:
options: ¹⁴⁷Sm → ¹⁴³Nd (α). T½ = 106 Ga. For mafic/ultramafic, REE minerals. εNd for mantle vs crust
questions.solution: Sm-Nd: immobile during alteration/metamorphism. Model ages (T_DM, T_CHUR). Crustal residence.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fold types by interlimb angle:
questions.solution: Interlimb angle: <30° tight, 0° isoclinal (parallel limbs).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal seam covers 2 km², is 3 m thick, with density 1.3 t/m³. The in-situ reserve is about:
questions.solution: Reserve = area×thickness×density = 2×10⁶ m²×3 m×1.3 t/m³ ≈ 7.8 Mt.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Groundwater recharge estimation:
options: Water table fluctuation, chloride mass balance, baseflow separation, isotope tracers
questions.solution: Recharge: critical for resource assessment. WTF: ΔS = S_y·ΔH. Chloride: P·Cl_p = R·Cl_gw.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Streak of chalcopyrite:
questions.solution: Chalcopyrite (CuFeS2) — brassy yellow. Most important copper ore mineral.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Mountain building — orogeny:
options: Convergent plate boundaries. Fold-thrust belts. Isostatic uplift
questions.solution: Orogeny: collision (Himalaya), Andean (subduction), accretional (terranes).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Thermal infrared remote sensing:
options: Emissivity and temperature. Night imaging. Fire detection. Urban heat island
questions.solution: TIR: 8-14 μm atmospheric window. Land surface temperature. Emissivity correction needed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Tunnel boring machine (TBM) selection depends on:
options: Rock mass strength, abrasivity, discontinuities, water inflow
questions.solution: TBM: hard rock (open), soft ground (shielded), mixed face (hybrid). CAI abrasivity index important.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Pyroclastic rocks:
options: Formed from volcanic ejecta (ash, lapilli, bombs, blocks)
questions.solution: Tuff (ash), lapillistone, agglomerate (bombs). Welded tuff = ignimbrite.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ordovician-Silurian extinction:
options: ~443 Ma. 85% of species lost. Gondwana glaciation. Sea level drop
questions.solution: End-Ordovician: two pulses. Hirnantian glaciation. Major effect on marine life.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Conglomerate:
questions.solution: Conglomerate: high energy environment (rivers, alluvial fans).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Shock metamorphism:
options: Meteorite impact. Very high P (GPa). Coesite, stishovite
questions.solution: Shock: impact craters. Shatter cones, planar deformation features in quartz.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Magnetic method — total intensity:
options: Aeromagnetic survey. Reduce to pole (RTP). Magnetic anomalies from susceptibility contrasts
questions.solution: Magnetic: iron-bearing rocks. IGRF correction. Diurnal variation. Mag: survey: nT precision.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Massive sulfide deposits typically contain:
questions.solution: VMS: formed at seafloor hydrothermal vents.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Re-Os dating of molybdenite:
options: Direct dating of mineral. Re substitutes for Mo. ¹⁸⁷Re → ¹⁸⁷Os
questions.solution: Molybdenite Re-Os: in-situ dating. No initial Os (Re/Os very high). Direct age of mineralization.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The angle that a bed makes with the horizontal, measured perpendicular to strike, is the:
questions.solution: Dip is the maximum inclination of a plane; strike is the horizontal line on it.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An aquifer (K = 50 m/day) of cross-section 500 m² has a hydraulic gradient of 0.01. The groundwater discharge is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Meanders:
options: Sinuous river bends. Cut bank (outside, erosion) + point bar (inside, deposition)
questions.solution: Meander: sinuosity > 1.5. Oxbow lake: cut-off meander. Neck cutoff.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Seismic hazard analysis: PSHA vs DSHA:
options: PSHA: probabilistic, all sources, annual probability. DSHA: deterministic, design earthquake
questions.solution: PSHA: hazard curves, return periods, exceedance probability. DSHA: single design earthquake.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A buried erosion surface separating older from much younger rocks is a(n):
questions.solution: An unconformity represents a gap (missing time) in the rock record.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Blueschist:
options: High P, low T. Glaucophane + lawsonite + jadeite
questions.solution: Blueschist: subduction zone metamorphism. Contains blue amphibole (glaucophane).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 3 m seam over 2 km² (density 1.4 t/m³) has a recovery factor of 0.85. The mineable reserve is about:
questions.solution: Mineable = area×thickness×density×recovery = 2M m²×3×1.4×0.85 ≈ 7.1 Mt.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Alluvial architecture:
options: Channel (sand/gravel) and overbank (mud) deposits. Connectivity affects flow
questions.solution: Channel: multistory, multilateral. Overbank: levees, crevasse splays, floodbasin. Heterogeneity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The law of superposition reliably gives relative age only when the strata are:
questions.solution: Tectonic overturning can invert beds, so superposition needs an undisturbed (right-way-up) sequence.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Borehole geophysics: electromagnetic induction log measures:
questions.solution: EM induction: no electrode contact. Works in non-conductive mud, air-filled holes.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Anorthosite:
questions.solution: Anorthosite: plagioclase-rich. Lunar highlands. Economic: Ti, Fe-Ti oxides.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An aquifer (K = 10 m/day) of cross-section 500 m² has a hydraulic gradient of 0.02. The groundwater discharge is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: TAS diagram for volcanic rocks: plots total alkali (Na₂O+K₂O) vs silica (SiO₂). Basalt field:
questions.solution: TAS: Le Bas et al. (1986). Basalt: 45-52% SiO₂. Andesite: 52-57%. Dacite: 63-69%. Rhyolite: >69%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Standard Penetration Test (SPT):
options: N-value: blows per 300mm. Correlated with strength (cohesionless soils)
questions.solution: SPT: 63.5kg hammer, 760mm drop. N₁(60): corrected for energy and overburden.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sheath fold:
options: Highly non-cylindrical. Like a knife sheath. Intense shear deformation
questions.solution: Sheath fold: sheath-like, eye-shaped in cross-section. Ductile shear zones.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A mineral specimen of mass 54 g has a volume of 30 cm³. Its specific gravity is about:
questions.solution: SG = mass/volume = 54/30 = 1.8.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Feldspathoid group — nepheline:
questions.solution: Nepheline: in silica-undersaturated rocks. Associated with alkali feldspar.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal sample (proximate analysis) has 5% moisture, 35% volatile matter and 20% ash. Its fixed carbon is:
questions.solution: Fixed C = 100 − (moisture + VM + ash) = 100 − (5+35+20) = 40%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fault — drag fold:
options: Folding adjacent to fault due to drag. Folds indicate relative motion
questions.solution: Drag fold: in fault zone. Normal drag vs reverse drag (antithetic). Help determine slip sense.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A mineral specimen of mass 105 g has a volume of 20 cm³. Its specific gravity is about:
questions.solution: SG = mass/volume = 105/20 = 5.25.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Tracer test determines:
questions.solution: Tracer: inject and monitor. Determines velocity, dispersivity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Coal rank indicators:
options: Vitrinite reflectance (Ro%), calorific value, volatile matter, carbon content
questions.solution: Ro% is most reliable. ASTM: uses Btu/lb and VM for classification. ISO: Ro%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An opencast block of 20000 m² has 20 m of overburden above the seam. The overburden volume to be removed is:
questions.solution: Volume = area × depth = 20000×20 = 400000 m³ = 0.4 million m³.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Molluscan shell mineralogy:
options: Aragonite (most), calcite (some), both (some). Not silica (except in diatoms)
questions.solution: Molluscs: aragonite common. Bivalves: aragonite or calcite. Gastropods: aragonite. Cephalopods: aragonite.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Spaceborne sensors — Landsat:
options: Landsat 8: OLI (9 bands, 30m) + TIRS (2 thermal, 100m). 16-day revisit. 185km swath
questions.solution: Landsat: 1972-2023. OLI bands: coastal, blue, green, red, NIR, SWIR1, SWIR2, pan (15m), cirrus.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Barakar Formation:
options: Permian coal measures in Gondwana. Major coal seams. Mineral matter low
questions.solution: Barakar: most important coal-bearing formation in India. Raniganj Formation also coal-bearing.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Groundwater geophysics — NMR:
options: Nuclear magnetic resonance (MRS). Direct detection of groundwater. T₂* decay time for pore size
questions.solution: MRS: surface NMR. Water content ε, decay T₂*. Logging NMR also. Porosity + permeability estimate.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Lineation:
options: Linear fabric element (mineral alignment, intersection, stretching)
questions.solution: Lineation: parallel to fold axes, mineral elongation, or intersection of foliations.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rock retains 25% of its original parent isotope (half-life 0.7 Gyr). Its radiometric age is about:
questions.solution: 25% remaining ⇒ 2 half-lives ⇒ age = 2×0.7 = 1.4 Gyr.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Metamorphic facies series: Barrovian type:
options: Medium P/T gradient (typical of continental collision). Kyanite → sillimanite
questions.solution: Barrovian: clockwise P-T path. Kyanite → sillimanite. Buchan: andalusite → sillimanite.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Migmatite:
options: Partially melted metamorphic rock. Dark (restite) + light (leucosome) bands
questions.solution: Migmatite: between metamorphism and igneous. Leucosome: granitic melt.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Amphibolite:
options: Hornblende + plagioclase. Medium-high grade. Equivalent to basalt/gabbro
questions.solution: Amphibolite: medium to high grade (amphibolite facies). No pyroxene (granulite has pyroxene).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal seam covers 1 km², is 5 m thick, with density 1.3 t/m³. The in-situ reserve is about:
questions.solution: Reserve = area×thickness×density = 1×10⁶ m²×5 m×1.3 t/m³ ≈ 6.5 Mt.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Remote sensing of mineralogy: ASTER bands for hydrothermal alteration:
options: SWIR bands (5,6,7,8) for clay, carbonate, sericite
questions.solution: ASTER SWIR: clay (band 6), carbonate (band 8), alunite (band 5). OH absorption features.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Rock mass rating (RMR): RMR = UCS+RQD+spacing+condition+groundwater. For granite with UCS=120MPa, RQD=80%, spacing=1m:
questions.solution: UCS=120→12, RQD=80→18, spacing=1m→20, condition→20, GW→15. Total ~85. Depends on exact ratings.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Metamorphic rocks form by the action of:
options: Heat and/or pressure on pre-existing rocks
questions.solution: Metamorphism alters mineralogy and texture in the solid state under heat/pressure.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An aquifer has hydraulic conductivity 10 m/day and a hydraulic gradient of 0.05. The Darcy velocity is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Seismic reflection:
options: Subsurface imaging by reflected P-waves. CMP stacking, migration. Resolution: λ/4 (~20m at 50 Hz)
questions.solution: Reflection: petroleum exploration. 2D, 3D seismic. Vibroseis (land), airgun (marine).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Rock bolt installation: grouted rock bolts provide:
options: Passive reinforcement (tension develops as rock moves)
questions.solution: Grouted bolts: resin or cement. Passive: load develops with rock movement. Full column grouting.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cambrian explosion:
options: Rapid appearance of major animal phyla (~541 Ma). Burgess Shale (507 Ma)
questions.solution: Burgess Shale (Canadian Rockies): exceptional preservation (soft parts). Trilobites, brachiopods.
